entry.title: Speedwagons
entry.summary: How many times can you stand up there and say the…
entry.content:
How many times can you stand up there and say the same thing over and over again?

Repetition is the key to excellence.

I get that.

But how many times must you get up and say the same crap, over and over?

And who can do that?

Who can have a hit song and still love to play it 50 years after its release?

I suppose the grateful can.

And the ones who like to hear themselves talk.

When I say the same thing over and over, it bores even me.

And if you're bored...

I've been called an AL of All Trades.

Master of none.

But I think that's telling.

I really get into a subject.

Like to listen to folks who chirp about it daily.

But at a point, I'm like:

This is the same sheet with different words and analogies.

Sometimes the same words and analogies.

Over and over.

How do they do that?

How do they grow?

How do they get better?

And what if they're wrong?

They're just so certain about it.

And they're still much better than the dude hawking a different thing every year.

Cryptos.

NFTs.

Stonks.

ai.

Openclaws.

Ugh.

The repeaters gotta be better than the fad chasers.

Right?

Who you got?

Same Old Same Old singing their one hit?

Or Bandwagon on the latest top 40 track?

REO.

🤷‍♂️

entry.title: Counting Up
entry.summary: I wish I could tell you that it will all work…
entry.content:
I wish I could tell you that it will all work out.

I wish I could tell you that if you do x, y, and z, that everything will come out the way you want it to.

But it doesn't.

So I can't.

When you survive something that others do not, it changes things.

I don't look through the lens of a non-survivor.

Everything is tinted with additional perspective.

All trauma is like that.

A stack of lenses.

In this increasingly obvious future of equalized intelligence, where even the lowest on our IQ totem pole will have apex tier access to global intellect, something interesting will be revealed.

If intelligence doesn't matter, or rather, is level across the board, what will make the difference?

Direction.

Curation.

Judgment.

And most of all, wisdom.

I'm wise enough to know I'm not wise at all.

The percentages do not matter.

The only experiments we truly care about are N = # of people you love. 

It can be N = 1. But hopefully, you've multiplied.

If 99% made it but you're part of the 1% that didn't, what's that worth?

And if 99% don't but you're part of the 1% that does, well that makes it better, right?

But I've come to peace with the knowledge that even if my number is called, there are worse fates.

Far worse.

Like the other numbers being called.

And while I know it in my bones, I'm not wise enough to know which choice is better.

Only that, if burdened with a choice, I will persevere.

I hope.

I pray.

But we all really don't have time to struggle with all this.

The timeline's the timeline.

And it's going to run out. For all of us.

On this one.

And that phone ain't gonna scroll itself.

At least, not yet.

🎶 1, 2, 3, 4...

Come on baby say you love me!

...5, 6, 7 times...

8, 9, 10, 11...

I'm just gonna keep on dancing! 🎵

entry.title: If It Costs Dollars, Make Sense
entry.summary: I got a rule in my house. Well, several but I'm…
entry.content:
I got a rule in my house.

Well, several but I'm here to talk about one.

One that you could use.

You should use.

Now, I'm not saying this rule with change your life.

In fact, it probably won't.

It'll just make you feel better.

Give you a sense of control.

And I suppose that's life-changing.

So the rule is simple: 

For every cent spent, some sense comes back.

It's become a game for me.

For every dollar you spend, expect at least some of it to come back to you.

For our purposes today, I want to talk about it in credit, but it should apply everywhere.

Any time you spend your money, make sure you get a nibble back.

It ain't a full bite, now, but it's something.

1%.

1.5%.

2%.

3%.

4%.

5%.

Yeah, you can get five on it on some sheet.

Most of you would do fine on just one card 2% back on everything.

I'm a bit of a nut so I maximize each category I can.

But it makes me happy to know things I would buy anyway (this is an important metric) gets me a little of it back.

Even debit cards are offering this stuff. But I like the intermediary of a credit card.

Remember, a little goes a long way.

And we always go long.

💳

entry.title: Around And Around We Go
entry.summary: This daily musings column may be becoming a weekly one. It's…
entry.content:
This daily musings column may be becoming a weekly one.

It's not that I don't love doing it, I do.

It's just that I am searching for things to say these days.

Or at least, not preparing to say it until I come here.

I just sits and types.

Let the thoughts flow through my fingers.

That's not a way to write a musings column.

I mean, I suppose it's one way, but it's not the way I want to write one (moving forward).

Bit of a funk.

Writer's block?

Muser's block?

Opportunity cost block?

Living up to the name of this place.

entry.title: Asymmetric Symmetry
entry.summary: We all love symmetry. Balance. Perfection. Order. But that doesn't mean…
entry.content:
We all love symmetry.

Balance.

Perfection.

Order.

But that doesn't mean we should dislike asymmetry.

In fact, many opposites of truth are not lies, but another truth.

Symmetry is perfect.

Asymmetry is imperfectly perfect.

I love asymmetry.

It's where all the juice is.

I'd like to think life is asymmetric.

20 seconds of insane courage can change your family tree.

That's staggering asymmetry.

We all love the curves of a perfect circle.

But we should also love the jagged edges of a market.

Up down. Up down. Up (hopefully).

Let's face it, you wouldn't be in the game if it only went up.

The downs keep you in.

Puts the cork in it.

On one side, we have harmony, balance, perfection.

On the other, we have fun house mirrors.

Which way to look?

Both are equal (even in its inequality).

Both are beautiful.

Both are true.

They are opposites.

And they attract.

To monster outcomes.

May your life be balanced with imbalance.

An imperfect infinity loop.

entry.title: Chill
entry.summary: It's hard to write on weekends. Hard is relative of course.
entry.content:
It's hard to write on weekends.

Hard is relative of course.

I just think today should be a rest day.

An off day.

Relaxed.

So, I won't write today.

Not a single word.

entry.title: How Much?
entry.summary: "There is nothing free in this world, but the grace of…
entry.content:
"There is nothing free in this world, but the grace of God."

Today, I want to talk about cost.

But not in the way you might be thinking.

Because sometimes, the most expensive things in our lives are free.

That is, they cost us the most.

The free things.

Now, some free things are cheap.

Some are priceless.

And some are worse than any loan you could ever sign.

In the business world, they talk about opportunity cost.

It's a fancy way of saying "Is this the best use of X? What do we lose out on by putting it here?"

In this case, X is money.

But X can be anything.

For if you put money here, you can't put it there.

Thus, there is lost.

That's the opportunity cost. You can't put it there, because you put it here.

It's a fine model.

One you should apply to your entire life, not just finances.

At what cost?

Is there a better place to put this (time, attention, energy)?

The answer is almost always a resounding "YES!"

You know what to do here.

And double down on all the times you say "No."

Do more of that.

They say if you have to ask how much it costs, you can't afford it.

But I say, like lots of things, they don't know what the fudge they talking about.

If you can afford it, always ask how much it costs.

You can't afford not to.

entry.title: Phatbergers
entry.summary: I got a confession to make. I want to help more…
entry.content:
I got a confession to make.

I want to help more people.

I really do.

But here's the thing:

I don't like people.

Most of them, anyway.

I'm sure I'm an assburger or whatever they call that blessing.

[Dramatic pause].

You seem alright, though.

I mean, you come here to read this sheet.

You're either crazy or cool.

Or both.

Either way, that's a good start.

I can work with that.

Just not now.

Give it time.

Until then, sorry folks, park's closed.

Sign outside should have told you.

But maybe, someday, I flip it over.

👐🍔

entry.title: Cyber and Space
entry.summary: So I'm dancing with ideas. 🎶 And we held the world,…
entry.content:
So I'm dancing with ideas.

🎶 And we held the world, we were turning it. 🎵

Like I do.

And it occurs to me that there are themes.

Genres.

In my life.

In my interests.

Iterating to Actualization.

Cyber and Space.

Quantum and Sacralization.

You ever wonder why they first called the internet and everything around it cyberspace?

It's this idea that there is so much in there (versus out).

A vastness.

With points of light in the mass of dark.

They say we analogize our brain with the latest technology.

So the brain is a computer, perhaps an ai one, and it is agentic to our soul.

And our universe is a cyberspace of sorts.

And if you really think about it, the only way to reach the real stars out there is to figure it all out inside these little boxes down here.

Cyber and Space.

💻🛸

To boldly go...[FARTS].

𖤂

entry.title: Fist on Chin
entry.summary: Welp. Today is/was a strange day. Sometimes, it be that way.
entry.content:
Welp.

Today is/was a strange day.

Sometimes, it be that way.

It occurs to me, that as little time as I put into this thing, I still need to have some focus.

And my mind is elsewhere.

Accumulating and processing data.

I never like reacting.

I'm pro active.

But this one, it's a thinker.

A best course of (re)actioner.

What's the correct response?

More important than a musings column.

At least for today.
